observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to secure at all times Sanguniang Bayan Resolution authorizing him to enter into contract with concerned contractors in behalf of the Municipality before perfection, for those projects under the lump-sum appropriation and fund transfers from various government agencies.

We recommended management to provide only enough appropriation for debt servicing to ensure optimum utilization of the development fund. Likewise, it is recommended to realign the excess appropriation to fund other priority projects that are economically beneficial to its constituents.

To achieve the objectives of RA 10121 and its IRR, we recommended the following courses of action:
1. Management must endeavor to implement disaster mitigation, preparedness and response capability programs and projects in accordance with its LDRRMF Investment Plan to effectively prevent the onslaught on lives and properties of the people of Compostela in times of natural disaster.
2. Maximization of available resources on the local DRRMF for disaster mitigation and preparedness in order to build the resilience of the Municipality of Compostela against disasters including climate change impacts.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the MEO and other concerned departments to closely monitor and ensure economic and efficient implementation of all projects under the pertinent National Programs by virtue of the fund transfers received from the National Government agencies for the implementation.

We recommended management to adhere with the documentary and reporting requirements of LBC 111 dated June 10, 2016 in order for them to identify and assess whether the existing LEEs are financially self-sufficient and profitable and provide them better decision making relative to the LEEs operations.

We recommended management to:
a. Require the Municipal Treasurer to identify the actual uncollected Real Property and Special Education Fund Taxes, excluding the penalties, to establish the balance of RPT/SET receivable as at yearend. Refer the issues and concerns about the program to the provider for them to be able to separate the computation of tax dues and penalties, update the system and correct the software errors if any.
b. Consequently, the Municipal Accountant is required to make the necessary adjustments to present fairly in the financial statements the balance of the said receivable accounts.
c. Require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile regularly the accounting and treasury records to verify the accuracy and validity of their records and immediately provide corrective action for discrepancies noted.

We recommended that the municipality’s development council headed by the Local Chief Executive shall ensure the optimum utilization of the Municipality’s 20% development fund. Likewise, we recommend that the management allocate the municipal budget properly to fully utilize the available resources to other important PPAs that are economically beneficial to its constituents.

To achieve the objectives of RA 10121 and its IRR, we recommended management to maximize the utilization of the LDRRMF from current year’s and continuing appropriations and prior years’ unexpended balance so that programs, projects and activities related to disaster prevention, mitigation, and preparedness program will be implemented within the scheduled timeframes. In such manner, the in-house capability to efficiently manage all types of emergencies and to achieve orderly transitions from response to sustained recovery in case of disaster/calamity will be strengthened to improve the disaster resilience of communities in the Municipality.

b. Identify the owners of properties under CARP and discuss with them the status of their properties and the settlement of their accountabilities.
c. Reconcile the records with the Municipal Assessor’s Office and update the software, if necessary.

Management Comment/s:
It is true that and correct that this Municipality procured goods and services under Small Value Procurement were not able to comply the posting requirement, 7 days prior the procurement under Section 54.2 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184. This is with the fact that our BAC Secretariat is undermanned. With this, we requested the Municipal Mayor to have an additional member and this was granted. Two members need to be trained on how to post in PhilGEPS. In addition, the BAC Secretariat has been tasked to post in the PhilGEPS all procurements under Small Value Procurement amounting to P50,000.00 and above 7 days prior to procurement.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the BAC Chairperson to establish a defined internal policy and clear procedures that includes the scheduling of the members of the BAC secretariat in taking turns in the posting in the PhilGEPS website and setting the maximum number of days the end users can submit PR before the goods/services are to be delivered so that the BAC will have ample time to do all the necessary procedures in the procurement through SVP.

Management Comment/s:
It is true and correct that this Municipality procured goods and services under Small Value Procurement without requiring the suppliers their PhilGEPS Registration Certificate which is in violation of Section 54.6 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.
It is sad to note that our local and other neighboring towns suppliers are not registered in PhilGEPS even how much our government encouraged them to register. They are having difficulties on the online registration. As much as this LGU wanted to patronize our own suppliers in return to the taxes they infuse to our coffers, we cannot do so.
With the truest and sincere regret, we accept our faults and we ask for a relief with the best interest in mind and that we will abide what is being mandated by law in our future dealings. With this in mind, starting early this year, we have implemented the “NO PHILGEPS REGISTRATION, NO GOVERNMENT TRANSACTIONS” policy.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the BAC Chairperson to evaluate their current procedures in the conduct of procurement through SVP and established a more defined internal policy and clear procedures that includes the persons responsible, man hours needed to accomplish, and the lists of documentary requirements needed, to avoid the awarding of contracts to suppliers who are not PhilGEPS registered and to achieve an efficient and effective procurement processes.

We recommended the management to require the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council to prepare the annual LDRRMF investment plan and incorporate the programs/projects/activities to be charged to the current year and prior years’ balances of local disaster risk reduction management fund (LDRRMF) pursuant to Section 4.4 of COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended management to:
Submit the authority granted by the Sangguniang Bayan for the specific purpose and disbursement of the fund for the generic projects mentioned. Failure to do so may result in audit disallowance.
Ensure that appropriation ordinances in generic terms are supported with an authorization from the Local Sanggunian approving the itemized appropriation of these funds.
See to it that infrastructure projects in the annual/supplemental budgets must be supported with Program of Work, Bill of Quantities and Detailed Cost Estimates, plans and specifications as the basis for its fund allocation for easy validation of project cost vis-à-vis accomplishment.

We recommended to the management the following courses of actions:
Review current workforce requirements of the Municipality vis-à-vis unimplemented PPAs. If the in-house capability is not adequate to implement PPAs as scheduled, consider the mode of implementation by contract for projects of substantial estimated project costs especially those where the target completion dates and/or year of implementation, have already lapsed; and
Create a monitoring team and closely follow through the periodic progress implementation of development projects especially those projects downloaded to the Barangays to ensure their completion within the programmed timelines; and
Strengthen the conduct of proper planning and timely execution of PPAs as well as continuous monitoring and evaluation of PPAs to address issues that may be needed to be resolved during their implementation.

We recommended that the municipality’s development council headed by the Local Chief Executive shall ensure the optimum utilization of the Municipality’s 20% development fund. Likewise, we recommend that the management allocate the municipal budget properly to fully utilize the available resources to other important PPAs that are economically beneficial to its constituents.

To achieve the objectives of RA 10121 and its IRR, we recommended management to maximize the utilization of the LDRRMF from current year’s and continuing appropriations and prior years’ unexpended balance so that programs, projects and activities related to disaster prevention, mitigation, and preparedness program will be implemented within the scheduled timeframes. In such manner, the in-house capability to efficiently manage all types of emergencies and to achieve orderly transitions from response to sustained recovery in case of disaster/calamity will be strengthened to improve the disaster resilience of communities in the Municipality.

We recommended the following courses of action:

The Local Chief Executive shall ensure the timely implementation of projects/programs under the 20% Development Fund and Local Funds. observations.recommendation:

Require the Municipal Property Inventory Team which should be composed of the representatives of the Municipal Mayor, Municipal Treasurer/ Municipal General Services Officer, Municipal Assessor and the Accountable Officer of every Department of the Municipal Government to conduct a complete physical inventory of all properties owned by the municipality at least once a year. Also, for properties without proper costing, the appraised value shall be the basis as recommended by the Appraisal Committee. All unserviceable equipment or properties should be included in the physical count and render a report thereon for possible disposal and/or dropping from the books of accounts.

Require the Municipal Government Services Office to prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RCPPE), and furnish the Municipal Accountant a copy of the report for reconciliation purposes in accordance to Section 124 of the NGAS. 

Direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal General Services Officer to maintain and update the necessary records on PPE as required in Section 114 of the NGAS Manual. Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Card shall be maintained by the Municipal Accountant and Property Cards by the Municipal General Services Officer. Periodic reconciliation of the accounting, general services records, and inventory reports of PPE should be undertaken. Any discrepancies noted, necessary adjustments shall be effected in order to present an accurate PPE balances in the financial statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the stale checks still in the hands of the treasurer should be cancelled and replaced in accordance with the guidelines set forth in Section 59 of NGAS Manual, Volume I.  Stale checks that are already released to the payees shall only be cancelled and replaced upon presentation thereof.  Meanwhile, the Treasurer should send a written notice to the payee to verify the existence of said checks.
observations.managementAction: Those checks could no longer be found which was in the possession of the former Treasurer Lorenzo Almodiel. Some stale checks were already cancelled and replaced especially those checks issued to barangays.

observations.recommendation: We  recommend that perfected contracts/ agreements, purchase orders and their supporting documents be submitted to COA within the reglementary period pursuant to Section 3.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001.
observations.managementAction: Not all perfected contracts/POs were submitted to COA as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2009-001 due to lack of manpower. Management assured that the recommendation will be complied in 2016.  The Budget Office staff will get copies of those documents for submission to your office.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant should exert extra effort to reconcile monthly the balance of cash in vault account between the general ledger and cashbook to correct immediately the difference noted in order to establish a reliable cash balance.

Likewise, collectors and designated liquidating officer should remit/turn over all collections at the end of the day to the municipal treasurer, so that the latter can deposit these collection with the authorized depository banks daily or not later than the next banking day pursuant to Section 32 of NGAS Manual Vol. I.
observations.managementAction: Due to voluminous works to attend it was not given a priority.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant should record the adjustments of all valid reconciling items to the respective Cash in Bank accounts in order to reflect the correct balances in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: There are still unrecorded vouchers not forwarded to this office during the time of former Lorenzo Almodiel which could not be located.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant should not allow the payment of transactions with incomplete supporting documents and the following documents should be submitted/ attached to the DVs: a). Pre inspection report to support on the procurement of materials in the repair of other structures, school building motor vehicles and IT equipments  b). Post repair inspection report for the payment of labor on the repair of other structures, school bldg., motor vehicle & IT equipment. c). Duly accomplished inspection and acceptance report on the deliveries of supplies and materials. d).   Detailed program of work on the repairs.  e). Approval of the Mayor-OIC on the disbursement vouchers.  f). Signature of the Municipal Accountant, Requisitioning Officer, Signature of the Budget Officer and the Municipal Treasurer on the DVs.  g). Original invoices in procurement of supplies & materials. h). Office orders authorizing the disbursing officer to draw/ open a Petty Cash Fund.  i). Travel order, cert. of appearance, & approved official list of athletes.  j). Remittance list/schedule k). List of recipients on meals and snacks serve during the in-house training & services rendered for peace & order.  l). Approved PO & PR m). Official Receipt.  Moreover, the practice of paying various claims with inadequate supporting documents should be stopped.
observations.managementAction: Those disbursement vouchers which were not submitted to the Municipal Accountant by the former Municipal Treasurer who was discharged from the service were still not supported with complete documentation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the accounting division should analyze those reconciling items and take up in the books the interest income and other reconciling items in order to reflect the correct account balances and ascertain the validity, propriety and legality of the disbursement.  The Municipal Treasurer should submit to the accounting office the encashed checks, disbursement vouchers and its supporting documents representing payments of expenses totaling P4,683,901.49 otherwise appropriate disciplinary actions should be instituted against the said official.  Upon receipt of the DVs the municipal accountant should record these unrecorded disbursements in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: Some of the reconciling items were already adjusted except for those vouchers which were not yet submitted to the accounting office by the previous Municipal Treasurer who was discharged from the service.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management:
a) Intensity collection efforts by sending demand or collection letters to the concerned debtors.
b)  Require officials & employees to liquidate their cash advances and the accountant to grant another cash advance once the previous are liquidated.
c) Institute measures deemed      appropriate to
ensure collection of the receivables.
d) Request authority form the Commission on 
Audit to write-off those accounts determined uncollectible and doubtful, pursuant to Section 36 of PD 1445.  The request shall be supported with a resolution from the Sangguniang Bayan and all evidences/proofs of the uncollectibility of said receivable accounts.
observations.managementAction: Some officials and employees had already made payments.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Accountant to immediately revert to surplus all accounts payable which are two (2) years or over pursuant to Section 159 of GAAM Volume I.
observations.managementAction: Still locating the source documents. Some payables were already reverted to surplus.

observations.recommendation: It is hereby recommended that concerned personnel should be required to refund the extra cash gifts totaling P1,568,000.00 and RATA differential totaling P51,610.00.  Henceforth, the Sangguniang Bayan and executive official should see to it that before appropriations are provided and expenditures are released for the implementation of RATA rates of a 3rd class municipality and extra cash gift, shall be strictly followed to prevent disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: Payments had been done thru payroll deduction of P50.00 per payday for each concerned officials and employees.

observations.recommendation: See to it that infrastructure projects in the annual/supplemental budgets must be supported with Program of Work, Bill of Quantities and Detailed Cost of Estimates, plans & specifications as basis for its fund allocation for easy validation of project cost vis-à-vis accomplishment.

Require the Municipal Social Welfare Officer/Municipal Agriculturist and the DSWD – Project Development Officer to fast track the implementation of the Sustainable Livelihood Programs in order to help the living condition of the 4Ps and “Typhoon Pablo” affected families.

Program funds downloaded by national government agencies with a prepared menu should carefully be evaluated before implementation to ensure its effectiveness. After careful evaluation, if programs are not viable, make representations to the donor agency for the realignment of program funds to other identified priority programs/projects of the municipality to avoid wastage of funds and to be able to attain the pro-poor objectives of the program.

It is recommended that the Philippine Councilor Leagues (PCL) and all other leagues organized by elected officials, to issue a government Official Receipts (OR)/Accountable Form No. 51 (AF 51) for all collections made so that proper financial accountability could be established.

It is recommended that the PCL collections should be deposited as trust fund and should be disbursed in accordance with the board of director’s resolutions, subject to the usual accounting and auditing rules and regulations.

It is recommended that all future registration fee payments for PCL conducted trainings/seminars/activities be receipted with a government official receipt which is Accountable Form It is recommended that the Philippine Councilor Leagues (PCL) and all other leagues organized by elected officials, to issue a government Official Receipts (OR)/Accountable Form No. 51 (AF 51) for all collections made so that proper financial accountability could be established.

It is recommended that the PCL collections should be deposited as trust fund and should be disbursed in accordance with the board of director’s resolutions, subject to the usual accounting and auditing rules and regulations.

It is recommended that all future registration fee payments for PCL conducted trainings/seminars/activities be receipted with a government official receipt which is Accountable Form.

We recommend to require the Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with the preparation and submission of the report on the sources and utilization of the LDRRMF to COA every end of each month pursuant to COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012, for verification/audit purposes.

We recommend to require the GSO to submit approved Purchase Orders together with their supporting documents to the Audit Team for contract review within five (5) working days from issuance as prescribed in Section 3.2.1 of COA Circular 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 in order to have a timely review and evaluation of the various contracts entered into by the Municipality. Also, see to it that Purchase Orders and other documents in the procurement process are duly accomplished, dated and received by concerned parties in compliance with the provisions of RA 9184.
Require the GAD Focal Person to submit the CY 2015 Annual Accomplishment Report to the COA Auditor to be able to validate and evaluate implemented programs, projects and activities.

Management through the GAD Focal Person shall seek approval on future GAD Plan and Budget and secure Certificate of Approval from the Department of Interior and Local Government (DILG) Regional office for the proper integration of the GAD Programs, Projects and activities in its Annual Investment Plan.

Likewise, the Municipal Planning and Development Office (MPDO) should establish a Gender database in order to have reliable and sufficient information for gender responsive planning, programming and policy formulation.

Management should require all accountable officers and employees with outstanding cash advances to immediately settle and/or liquidate their respective accountability, pursuant to COA Circular 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 and Section 89 of PD 1445.


In the event that the officials and employees concerned still fail to settle/liquidate their cash advances, appropriate malversation charge may be filed in accordance with Articles 217 and Article 218 of the Revised Penal Code or criminal prosecution under Section 128 of P.D. No. 1445 as provided under Section 9 of COA Circular No. 2012-004 dated November 28, 2012.


Further, file appropriate Administrative Proceedings for Gross Neglect of Duty punishable by dismissal from the service for the officers and employees who failed to liquidate the cash advance or Simple Neglect of Duty punishable by suspension from the service for those who failed to liquidate the cash advances in full within the prescribed given periods pursuant to Civil Service Commission MC No. 12, s. 2012.
observations.reason: As of December 31, 2016, the P11,598,328.31 unliquidated cash advances as of December 31, 2014 had a remaining balance of only P531,895.26, Thus, P11,066,433.05 were liquidated for the past years. Some remaining amount pertained to cash advances granted to persons who are no longer in service.

observations.recommendation:
Strengthen the GAD Focal Point System and build capabilities of the agency on GAD thru trainings.


Require the GAD Focal Point System to conduct gender analysis to be able to incorporate the programs/activities/projects (PAPs) that address gender issues in the GAD plan and budget as well as its mainstreaming in the agency operations.


Provide an allocation of at least 5% of the total budget for GAD related activities pursuant to DBM-NEDA-PCW Joint Circular Nos. 2004-1 and 2012-01.


Implement the PAPs embodied in the GAD plan and monitor and evaluate the agency’s gender mainstreaming and women’s empowerment efforts through the GAD Focal Person.
observations.reason: The GAD Focal Point System are still on the conduct of trainings of agency personnel for them to be able to identify gender issues and concerns and incorporate the same in the plan and its mainstreaming in the agency operations.
